rulings.Case Title: ADRIANA HIX et al VS. ST.MARY'S MEDICAL CENTER et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 20, 2009, line 1, PLAINTIFFS ADRIANA HIX, and, LARRY HIX MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Motion denied. Triable issue of fact raised whether SFAMG should be vicariously liable for Dr. Jain's medical negligence, if any. Partnership agreement and Stringer declaration state the partners of SFAMG are medical corporations, not individuals. Triable issue of fact raised whether SFAMG is vicariously liable for any negligence of Defendant Jain as an employee of a partner. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: SHARI NADAL VS. LOUIS VUITTON NORTH AMERICA, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Separate Trials Of Issues; Memorandum Of P/A In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 20, 2009, line 3, DEFENDANTS LOUIS VUITTON NORTH AMERICA, INC., and, TERANY JACKSON Motion For Separate Trials Of Issues. Motion for separate trials of issues (bifurcation) granted in part and denied in part. Granted as to amount of punitive damages. Denied as to remainder of issues without prejudice to re-raising the motion before the trial judge. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: SHARI NADAL VS. LOUIS VUITTON NORTH AMERICA, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 20, 2009, line 3, DEFENDANTS LOUIS VUITTON NORTH AMERICA, INC., and, TERANY JACKSON MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for summary judgment denied. Motion for summary adjudication denied as to first cause of action for battery and the second cause of action for assault. Triable issue of fact raised whether Jackson had the requisite intent to injure plaintiff and whether plaintiff was in reasonable apprehension of Jackson before the contact. Summary adjudication denied as to third cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress. Triable issue of fact whether Jackson's conduct extreme and outrageous. Plaintiff claims Jackson harassed and physically struck her. Triable issue of fact whether Louis Vuitton's conduct after the incident extreme and outrageous and contributed to plaintiff's emotional injury. Summary adjudication granted as to fourth and eighth causes of action for wrongful termination and violation of the Labor Code, sixth cause of action for retaliation, and ninth cause of action for disability discrimination. No triable issue of fact raised. Plaintiff's counsel advised Louis Vuitton that plaintiff was physically incapable of returning to work and plaintiff did not respond to defendant's injury into what reasonable accommodation it could provide. Summary adjudication granted as to seventh cause of action for breach of implied covenant of good faith and fifth cause of action for breach of contract. No triable issue of material fact raised. Plaintiff has not plead an enforceable contract and cannot rebut the presumption of at-will employment. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: BRIAN J. HECKTMAN et al VS. BING LOUIE LOUIE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause Regarding Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 20, 2009, line 4, Order to Show cause regarding preliminary injunction. Motion for preliminary injunction denied. Insufficient evidence presented that defendants have a reasonable probability of prevailing on the merits. The balance of equities and harm favors plaintiffs who have spent almost $200,000 on this project, plus $1,600 per day until the project is completed and $2,000 per week for two rented bobcats. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: FREMONT BANK A CALIFORNIA BANKING CORPORATION VS. BOEGER THOMAS MUTLIMEDIA A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 20, 2009, line 5, PLAINTIFF FREMONT BANK MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for summary judgment granted as to Christopher Harmon individually, but denied without prejudice for attorney fees. Motion for summary judgment denied as to defendants Christopher and Maggie Harmon as Trustee, Boeger Thomas Multimedia, Boeger and Thomas against whom defaults have been entered. Default judgments are set in Department 218, not the Law and motion departments. Request for attorney fees may be made by noticed motion in Department 302. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ARTHUR WEBB VS. WORKERS' COMPENSATION APPEALS BOARD et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 20, 2009, line 7, DEFENDANT WORKERS' COMPENSATION APPEALS BOARD, RONNIE CAPLANE, JAMES CUENO, WILLIAM O'BRIEN, NEIL SULLIVAN, and, DAVID HETTICK DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Is sustained without leave to amend. No opposition filed. Court has no jurisdiction over plaintiff's complaint. WCAB has exclusive jurisdiction. Labor Code section 5300 et seq; Vacanti vs. SCIF (2001) 24 Cal. 4th 800, 818. =(302/CWW)
